opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My right ear is burning hot, completely swollen, and the pain is so intense I can't rest my head on the pillow. I think I have a severe infection.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It flared badly over the evening and I'm scared it's spreading or I'll get a cauliflower ear.

symptom_narrative.character: red, hot, swollen, exquisitely tender upper ear
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: intense, can't lie on that side
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: upper (cartilage) part of the right ear
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: the earlobe is NOT involved; no fever; no ear-canal discharge; no hearing loss; no recent piercing/injury; no pain behind the ear over the bone

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: episodic inflammation of the ear cartilage (a cartilage-inflammation condition)

objective_data_offered.value: the upper cartilage part of the right ear is red, swollen and hot; the fleshy earlobe at the bottom looks completely normal; no discharge from the ear canal; behind the ear (over the bone) isn't swollen or sticking out
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: phone-camera view; cannot palpate the mastoid, examine the ear canal/drum, or exclude perichondritis definitively
